needle_load.note: Claude adds a 5s 'idle' phase; even combined idle+load=10s, GPT-4o still reads ~5s longer here.

second_throw.note: LARGEST per-phase delta in the V-series. GPT-4o places the entire setup-and-cinch into second_throw; Claude segments it earlier.

third_throw.note: Second largest per-phase delta. GPT-4o keeps all late tail management inside third_throw; Claude closes third_throw at 100s and moves to cutting.

suture_cut.note: Mirror image: Claude assigns 45s to cutting, GPT-4o only 6.7s. Sum of third_throw+cut: Claude 67s vs GPT-4o 56s (delta 11s, closer).

hand_identification.note: Claude could not identify leading hand at all; GPT-4o committed to R/L/R. Both agree the alternation occurred.

gap_visible.note: AGREEMENT on drain — breaks the V11/V14/V16/V17 divergence pattern. V18 joins V12 and V13 as agreement cases.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: V18 is the widest FLS disagreement in the V-series (Δ17.4). Both teachers agree on every qualitative rubric item — knots correct, hands alternated, no avulsion, no visible gap, placement within 3.5-4.0mm. The entire delta is attributable to WHERE each teacher marked completion on the timeline: Claude at ~145s (drain closed, tails short, instruments withdrawn — frame 23), GPT-4o at ~162.9s (cutter still dividing tails at frame 4957). GPT-4o has higher confidence (0.64 vs 0.43) and explicitly timestamps a cutter-active frame at 165.2s, which argues its endpoint is more literal. Claude's 145s likely corresponds to when the knot was visually complete but before cutter-stop. Midpoint 442.3 is the safest reconciled value; if forced to pick one, GPT-4o's 433.6 has the stronger evidence chain.

systematic_findings.endpoint_definition_disagreement: V18 exposes a new systematic issue: when does completion end? Claude appears to use 'knot complete + tails trimmed + instruments withdrawn' as the stop condition, GPT-4o uses 'cutter finishes dividing tails'. On short clips this is small (V17 had a 2.8s delta). On V18 it opens to 17.9s because tail management after the knot was unusually extended. RECOMMENDATION: for training data, standardize the rubric's 'completion' definition — 'last frame in which the cutting instrument is dividing suture material inside the trainer' is the cleanest, most reproducible anchor. This would align GPT-4o and gently pull Claude's numbers down on V-series videos with long tail-management tails.
systematic_findings.drain_gap_pattern_broken_again: V18 joins V12 and V13 as cases where both teachers agree on closure. Running count of V-series drain-gap calls: AGREE (no gap): V12, V13, V18. DIVERGE (Claude sees gap, GPT-4o doesn't): V11, V14, V16, V17. The monitor-recording bias hypothesis needs refinement — it's not deterministic on recording format alone.
systematic_findings.phase_boundary_issue_amplified: V18 has both the widest per-phase deltas seen in the series (second_throw Δ29.9s, third_throw Δ27.3s, cut Δ38.3s) AND near-perfect rubric agreement. This is the clearest possible demonstration that phase durations are the wrong training signal — total_time + penalties converges much better than any phase-level metric.
